tool_name: Single Pile Axial Capacity — Shaft Friction, Base Resistance & EC7 Design Verification

inputs.name: Pile Shaft Diameter (D)
inputs.note: Standard CFA: 300–900 mm; driven steel H: 250–360 mm; bored cast-in-situ: 300–1500 mm
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 IT5 — Precision grinding tolerance: 4-18µm (≈0.5% for Ø10-50mm)

inputs.name: Pile Construction Method

options.label: Driven steel H-pile — β method in sand

inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Dominant Soil Profile
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Soil Strength Parameter — c_u (kPa) for clay OR N_60 for sand
inputs.note: Clay: c_u from UU triaxial or vane shear; Sand: N_60 from SPT (energy-corrected)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Shaft Adhesion / Friction Factor (α or β)
inputs.note: Clay α: 0.4–0.5 bored, 0.5–0.6 driven; Sand β: 0.2–0.4; EC7 Annex D / FHWA NHI-16-064
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Unit Base (End-Bearing) Resistance (q_b)
inputs.note: Clay: q_b = N_c × c_u = 9 × c_u; Dense sand: q_b = 3000–8000 kPa; Rock: 10,000–20,000 kPa
inputs.uncertainty_source: IEC 61298-2:2008 §4.2 — Transducer accuracy class 1.0: ±1% of span

inputs.name: Partial Resistance Factor for Pile (γ_R,t)

options.label: γ_R,t = 1.3 — Driven pile, model factor correlated

inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: D_m = pile_diameter_mm / 1000; perimeter_m = Math.PI * D_m; Ab_m2 = Math.PI / 4 * Math.pow(D_m, 2)  // [m, m²] — Pile Shaft Perimeter & Base Area — Ref: Geometric properties — perimeter for shaft friction integration; base area for end bearing

fs_kpa = (soil_type === 'clay') ? alpha_adhesion * cu_or_N60_value : alpha_adhesion * cu_or_N60_value * 10; Qs_kn = fs_kpa * perimeter_m * pile_length_m  // [kN] — Total Shaft (Skin) Friction Resistance (Q_s) — Ref: Q_s = α·c_u·π·D·L (clay α-method) OR β·σ'_v·π·D·L (sand β-method) — EC7 Annex D; FHWA NHI-16-064 §3.3
Qb_kn = qb_kpa * Ab_m2  // [kN] — Base (End-Bearing) Resistance (Q_b) — Ref: Q_b = q_b × A_b — EC7 §7.6.2.3; q_b from correlation or field test (CPT/PLT)
Rck_kn = Qs_kn + Qb_kn  // [kN] — Characteristic Pile Resistance (R_c,k) — Ref: R_c,k = Q_s + Q_b — EC7 §7.6.2.1; characteristic value from calculation (model factor included in γ_R)

Rcd_kn = Rck_kn / gamma_R_t; mob_factor = (pile_type === 'bored') ? 0.9 : 1.0; Rcd_mob_kn = Rcd_kn * mob_factor  // [kN] — Design Pile Resistance (R_c,d) & Settlement-Limited Mobilisation — Ref: R_c,d = R_c,k / γ_R,t — EC7 §7.6.2.3; mobilisation factor 0.9 for bored piles (full shaft mobilisation at 1% D)

eta_group = (pile_type === 'clay') ? Math.min(1.0, 0.7 + 0.1 * Math.log10(pile_length_m / D_m)) : 1.0; R_group_kn = 4 * Rcd_mob_kn * eta_group  // [dimensionless, kN] — Pile Group Efficiency (for 2×2 group estimate) — Ref: Group efficiency η < 1 in clay (block failure): EC7 §7.6.2.5 / Converse-Labarre; sand groups η ≈ 1.0

rules.message: Design resistance is negative: check soil strength input — minimum c_u or N_60 must be positive and realistic
L/D < 8: stubby pile — shaft friction contribution is limited; this behaves more as a spread footing; verify end bearing dominates
Unit base resistance exceeds 15 × c_u: unrealistic for clay — theoretical maximum N_c = 9 for deep piles; cap q_b at 9 × c_u per Skempton (1951)

smart_warnings.message: End bearing > 70% of shaft: base-dominant pile — settlement at working load may be 30–50 mm before full base mobilisation; consider longer pile to increase shaft contribution
Bored pile in soft clay (c_u < 40 kPa): construction disturbance reduces α — use α = 0.4 maximum; verify bentonite support and concrete workability
Driven precast pile D > 900 mm: driving damage risk is severe for large diameters — switch to bored, CFA, or barrette; driving stresses per GRLWEAP analysis mandatory
Long bored pile with standard γ_R: consider pile testing (static or dynamic CAPWAP) to validate shaft friction distribution — testing permits γ_R reduction to 1.3 per EC7 §7.6.3.3
Group efficiency < 85% in clay: block failure may govern — calculate block capacity per EC7 §7.6.2.5; Qblock = c_u × (perimeter_block × L + Ablock × 9)

fmea.effect: Design resistance is negative: check soil strength input — minimum c_u or N_60 must be positive and realistic
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: L/D < 8: stubby pile — shaft friction contribution is limited; this behaves more as a spread footing; verify end bearing dominates
Unit base resistance exceeds 15 × c_u: unrealistic for clay — theoretical maximum N_c = 9 for deep piles; cap q_b at 9 × c_u per Skempton (1951)

fmea.failureMode: End bearing > 70% of shaft: base-dominant pile — settlement at working load may be 30–50 mm before full base mobilisation; consider longer pile to increase shaft contribution
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Qb_kn > Qs_kn * 0.7

fmea.failureMode: Bored pile in soft clay (c_u < 40 kPa): construction disturbance reduces α — use α = 0.4 maximum; verify bentonite support and concrete workability
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: pile_type === 'bored' && cu_or_N60_value < 40

fmea.failureMode: Driven precast pile D > 900 mm: driving damage risk is severe for large diameters — switch to bored, CFA, or barrette; driving stresses per GRLWEAP analysis mandatory
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: pile_diameter_mm > 900 && pile_type === 'driven_concrete'

fmea.failureMode: Long bored pile with standard γ_R: consider pile testing (static or dynamic CAPWAP) to validate shaft friction distribution — testing permits γ_R reduction to 1.3 per EC7 §7.6.3.3
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: gamma_R_t === 1.5 && pile_length_m > 30

fmea.failureMode: Group efficiency < 85% in clay: block failure may govern — calculate block capacity per EC7 §7.6.2.5; Qblock = c_u × (perimeter_block × L + Ablock × 9)
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: eta_group < 0.85 && soil_type === 'clay'
